title: Explainable Human-in-the-Loop Dynamic Data-Driven Digital Twins
authors: Zhang N. / Bahsoon R. / Tziritas N. / Theodoropoulos G.
karpathy_what: Combines XAI with human-in-the-loop control inside a dynamic data-driven digital twin simulation framework.
karpathy_why_it_matters: The single paper closest to the thesis. Proves the HITL+CDT+XAI combination is viable as a research object. Provides vocabulary for dynamic state modelling under uncertainty.
karpathy_gap_left: Focuses on simulation fidelity — not on structured human decision authority with override rights or SLA-aware routing. Human is observer not governance authority.
thesis_relevance: Foundational reference for Chapter 2 and Chapter 3. Directly cited in gap statement. Architecture extends this.

title: A Digital Twin Framework for Enhancing Human–Agentic AI–Machine Collaboration
authors: Nicoletti B. / Appolloni A.
karpathy_what: Proposes a layered digital twin architecture where human operators agentic AI and machines co-exist in a structured collaboration framework.
karpathy_why_it_matters: Establishes that digital twin frameworks can formally accommodate human–AI collaboration roles. Closest architectural antecedent to the HITL-CDT prototype.
karpathy_gap_left: Treats the human as one actor among equals — not as a governance authority with structured override rights and SLA-bound escalation paths. No empirical evaluation of decision outcomes.
thesis_relevance: Directly informs System Design (Chapter 4). Architecture comparison references this.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Digital Twins: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Kreuzer T. / Papapetrou P. / Zdravkovic J.
karpathy_what: SLR confirming that the dominant pattern in digital twin AI research is predictive modelling and monitoring — not structured human decision integration or governance.
karpathy_why_it_matters: The SLR itself is gap evidence. Confirms the field has been systematically reviewed and found to lack structured human decision authority integration — a strong claim.
karpathy_gap_left: Being an SLR it synthesises rather than solves. Confirms the gap exists but does not propose a design to fill it.
thesis_relevance: Key citation in gap statement (Chapter 2). Literature review table references Kreuzer taxonomy.

title: The Emergence of Cognitive Digital Twin: Vision Challenges and Opportunities
authors: Zheng X. / Lu J. / Kiritsis D.
karpathy_what: Defines the cognitive digital twin as an evolution beyond traditional digital twins — adding reasoning learning and decision-support capabilities to the standard simulation model.
karpathy_why_it_matters: Provides the theoretical definition of CDT that the thesis builds on. Establishes CDT as a research-worthy concept distinct from standard digital twins.
karpathy_gap_left: Visionary paper — does not implement a HITL governance layer or empirical evaluation.
thesis_relevance: Chapter 2 CDT definition section. The thesis artifact is positioned as an implementation of the CDT vision.

title: Cognitive Twins for Supporting Decision-Makings of Internet of Things Systems
authors: Lu J. / Zheng X. / Gharaei A. / Kalaboukas K. / Kiritsis D.
karpathy_what: Applies cognitive twin concepts to IoT decision support — demonstrates a working cognitive twin that recommends actions based on system state.
karpathy_why_it_matters: Concrete CDT implementation precedent. Shows cognitive twins can support operational decisions not just monitoring.
karpathy_gap_left: IoT domain — not enterprise data operations. No HITL or human override mechanism.
thesis_relevance: Chapter 2 CDT applications section.

title: Cognitwin: Hybrid and Cognitive Digital Twins for the Process Industry
authors: Abburu S. / Berre A.J. / Jacoby M. / Roman D. / Stojanovic L. / Stojanovic N.
karpathy_what: Introduces the Cognitwin architecture — a hybrid CDT combining physical simulation with cognitive reasoning for process industry applications.
karpathy_why_it_matters: One of the first concrete CDT implementations. Validates that cognitive reasoning can be embedded in digital twins in an industrial context.
karpathy_gap_left: Process industry domain. No HITL or human decision authority — cognitive layer is fully automated.
thesis_relevance: Chapter 2 CDT section. Historical precedent for cognitive reasoning in digital twins.

title: Cognitive Digital Twin for Manufacturing Systems
authors: Al Faruque M.A. / Muthirayan D. / Yu S.-Y. / Khargonekar P.P.
karpathy_what: Demonstrates a cognitive digital twin for manufacturing — uses ML to predict equipment failures and recommend maintenance actions.
karpathy_why_it_matters: Manufacturing CDT precedent. Connects ML-based prediction to digital twin state updates.
karpathy_gap_left: Manufacturing domain. No HITL — recommendations are automated without human override.
thesis_relevance: Chapter 2 CDT applications. Cross-domain validation of CDT concept.

karpathy_why_it_matters: Directly relevant because your ML model is a RandomForest. TreeSHAP is the specific SHAP variant used. Nature Machine Intelligence venue gives strong credibility.
karpathy_gap_left: Algorithm paper — no HITL or operational context.
